title: The Merv Griffin Show

jokes.text: Yeah, you know, Cain slew Abel. No, he didn't. They were in business together. Dry wall or something.
jokes.setup: Jerry correctly states the biblical story of Cain and Abel
jokes.punchline: Kramer confidently misremembers it as a construction business partnership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer moment - confidently wrong about basic knowledge in a hilariously specific way

jokes.text: Well, I think Abel worked hard all summer harvesting his crops... while Cain just played in the field. Then when winter came, Abel had all the nuts, Cain had no nuts.
jokes.setup: Kramer continues his biblical revision
jokes.punchline: Reduces the first murder to a seasonal nut dispute
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity nicely, though 'nuts' pun feels slightly forced

jokes.text: The way I remember it, Cain, he was a successful doctor... but when he took this special formula, he became Mr. Abel.
jokes.setup: Third attempt at biblical revision
jokes.punchline: Conflates Cain and Abel with Jekyll and Hyde
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation that perfectly caps the sequence

jokes.text: Yeah. Well, I was trying to make gravel and it just... It just didn't work out.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks why Kramer broke his blender
jokes.punchline: Kramer matter-of-factly explains he was making gravel in a kitchen appliance
jokes.explanation: Peak Kramer absurdity - treating an impossible task as merely unsuccessful

jokes.text: Well, I like the sound it makes when you walk on it.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks why he was making gravel
jokes.punchline: Kramer's innocent, childlike reason for the impossible task
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but less surprising after the gravel revelation

jokes.text: This stuff belongs in the Smithsonian. Or at least in the Dumpster behind the Smithsonian.
jokes.setup: Kramer elevates the TV set's historical importance
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates it with the dumpster qualifier
jokes.explanation: Well-structured self-deflating joke with good economy

jokes.text: Boy, one minute Elliott Gould is sitting on you... and the next thing you're yesterday's trash.
jokes.setup: Kramer philosophizes about the furniture's fall from grace
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes the chair's celebrity experience
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about celebrity culture and obsolescence

jokes.text: You know, I spilled a yogurt smoothie in here two days ago.
jokes.setup: Context of them examining an old TV set chair
jokes.punchline: Jerry casually mentions contaminating what Kramer treats as an artifact
jokes.explanation: Mild contrast between Jerry's practicality and Kramer's reverence

jokes.text: George, watch out for those pigeons. Oh, they'll get out of the way. You really smell banana?
jokes.setup: Jerry warns George about pigeons while driving
jokes.punchline: George dismisses concern, immediately distracted by banana smell
jokes.explanation: Sets up the pigeon running gag but not particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: You're making this up. There was never a Joseph Garnish.
jokes.setup: Someone claims garnish was invented by Joseph Garnish
jokes.punchline: Elaine flatly rejects the obvious fake etymology
jokes.explanation: Simple pun rejection, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I inherited them after he died from a long, painful bout with... SuperBall.
jokes.setup: Serious tone about inheriting toys after father's death from illness
jokes.punchline: The 'illness' is a toy SuperBall
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from serious to absurd, well-structured

jokes.text: They've never been played with. I just wanna touch them a little.
jokes.setup: Celia explains the toys are valuable because they're untouched
jokes.punchline: Jerry's desperate, almost inappropriate pleading to touch them
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Jerry's character arc, mildly uncomfortable tone

jokes.text: Hey, your coffee stain looks like Fidel Castro. You've been an enormous help.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks Walter about the new guy
jokes.punchline: Walter's useless observation and Elaine's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Decent workplace humor showing character frustration

jokes.text: Don't we have a deal with the pigeons? Of course we have a deal. They get out of the way of cars... we look the other way on the statue defecation.
jokes.setup: George asks if there's a deal with pigeons
jokes.punchline: Jerry elaborates an imaginary social contract with specific terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise treated with legal precision - very quotable

jokes.text: So he just sidles up? That's right. He's a real sidler.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks for clarification about the coworker's behavior
jokes.punchline: Elaine coins 'sidler' as a noun for this specific type of person
jokes.explanation: Creates a new Seinfeld neologism in classic style

jokes.text: I was sitting making Cup-a-Soup... singing that song from The Lion King. 'Hakuna Matata'? I thought I was alone.
jokes.setup: Elaine describes being caught in an embarrassing moment
jokes.punchline: The specific embarrassment of singing Disney songs while making instant soup
jokes.explanation: Relatable workplace embarrassment with specific details

jokes.text: To me, the 'Hakuna Matata' is not nearly as embarrassing... as the Cup-a-Soup.
jokes.setup: Elaine's embarrassment about being caught singing
jokes.punchline: Jerry finds the instant soup more shameful than Disney songs
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observation that reframes the situation unexpectedly

jokes.text: Oh, you just bring it in sideways and hook it.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks how Kramer got the huge TV set into his apartment
jokes.punchline: Kramer treats the logistically impossible as merely technical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer casual dismissal of obvious problems

jokes.text: A lot of the stars from the '70s... they were not as hygienic as they appeared on TV. You take Mannix, for example.
jokes.setup: Jerry complains the chair smells like garbage
jokes.punchline: Kramer's specific conspiracy theory about 70s TV star hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist theory with specific reference that makes it funnier

jokes.text: Is it possible you're even more beautiful... than the last time I saw you?
jokes.setup: Kramer hosting his talk show
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top talk show host flattery delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Shows Kramer's commitment to the talk show bit but fairly standard

jokes.text: I'm reading the fascinating article... on the most fascinating people of the year. And done.
jokes.setup: Peterman claims to be reading while Elaine waits
jokes.punchline: He finishes the 'fascinating' article instantly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Peterman's pretentious but shallow nature

jokes.text: An Army Pete. He was made of wood, in the rain he would swell up and then split.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jerry's childhood toy deprivation
jokes.punchline: The pathetic details of his knockoff G.I. Joe
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory with specific absurd details

jokes.text: And we all know how painful that can be.
jokes.setup: Jerry describes his childhood toy trauma
jokes.punchline: Kramer's faux-therapeutic acknowledgment as talk show host
jokes.explanation: Kramer committing to talk show therapist role

jokes.text: Excuse me? Yeah, we're talking this way.
jokes.setup: Elaine tries to have normal conversation
jokes.punchline: Kramer insists on maintaining talk show format even off-camera
jokes.explanation: Shows Kramer's total commitment to his delusion

jokes.text: Now, for those of us who don't know, sidling is what?
jokes.setup: Elaine mentions sidling problem
jokes.punchline: Kramer asks for definition like a professional host
jokes.explanation: Continues the talk show commitment but not particularly strong

jokes.text: You sidle? You stomp around like a Clydesdale.
jokes.setup: Kramer claims he'll sidle the sidler
jokes.punchline: Elaine points out Kramer's notoriously heavy footsteps
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation with great specific imagery

jokes.text: Not with these honeys. Wrestling shoes. Only in New York.
jokes.setup: Elaine says Kramer can't sidle
jokes.punchline: Kramer's solution is wrestling shoes with New York pride
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer logic and the random New York pride is characteristic

jokes.text: What happened? I'll ask the questions.
jokes.setup: George starts to explain what happened
jokes.punchline: Kramer cuts him off to maintain talk show host authority
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic joke showing Kramer's delusion

jokes.text: Now, you and Jerry dated for a while. Tell us... what was that like?
jokes.setup: George is on the talk show
jokes.punchline: Kramer asks George about dating Jerry as if it happened
jokes.explanation: Awkward assumption creates good cringe moment

jokes.text: So they're like everyone else.
jokes.setup: George complains pigeons are ignoring him
jokes.punchline: Kramer matter-of-factly notes this puts pigeons in line with everyone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan burn with excellent economy

jokes.text: Then why don't you just run it over too?
jokes.setup: George complains about an inaccurate bank clock
jokes.punchline: Jerry sarcastically suggests George run over the clock too
jokes.explanation: Good callback but fairly predictable sarcasm

jokes.text: What pigeon? You drove right into that squirrel.
jokes.setup: George claims he saved a pigeon's life by swerving
jokes.punchline: Jerry reveals George actually hit a squirrel instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that escalates George's delusion

jokes.text: Squirrel? Well, we have no deal with them.
jokes.setup: George realizes he hit a squirrel instead of avoiding a pigeon
jokes.punchline: George immediately shifts to justifying it based on the 'deal' logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect George rationalization that extends the pigeon deal premise

jokes.text: 'Will not cause drowsiness.' 'May cause drowsiness.'
jokes.setup: Celia asks for aspirin for headache
jokes.punchline: Jerry deliberately chooses the wrong medicine by reading labels
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jerry's scheme but feels slightly contrived

jokes.text: Unfortunately, I am also disgusted.
jokes.setup: Peterman praises Elaine's work speed
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses with disgust at the quality
jokes.explanation: Good Peterman misdirection with characteristic dramatic delivery

jokes.text: Mission accomplished. Back to base, Joe.
jokes.setup: Jerry playing with G.I. Joe in sink while Celia is unconscious
jokes.punchline: Jerry doing full military roleplay with the action figure
jokes.explanation: Shows Jerry's childish regression but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, it's by the pound, so about 80 cents.
jokes.setup: George asks cost of putting squirrel to sleep
jokes.punchline: Vet casually quotes cheap euthanasia price by weight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: So they're flying the tiny instruments in from El Paso.
jokes.setup: Context of expensive squirrel surgery
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about importing miniature surgical tools
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation of the premise

jokes.text: El Paso. I spent a month there one night.
jokes.setup: George mentions El Paso
jokes.punchline: Newman's classic one-liner about a bad experience there
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy structure, well-delivered but not original

jokes.text: You mean to say that you drugged a woman... so you could take advantage of her toys?
jokes.setup: Jerry explains getting to play with toys while Celia was unconscious
jokes.punchline: Kramer's horrified reframing makes it sound much worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent reframing that creates maximum cringe through implication

jokes.text: What you do with your personal life is your business. But when you're on my set, you clean it up, mister.
jokes.setup: Jerry's drugging confession creates scandal
jokes.punchline: Kramer takes moral high ground as talk show host
jokes.explanation: Good character commitment to host persona with moral outrage

jokes.text: You're spontaneous. You're symmetrical.
jokes.setup: Elaine complimenting Lou before addressing his problem
jokes.punchline: Symmetrical as a compliment about physical appearance
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Elaine's awkward diplomacy

jokes.text: Thanks, Elaine. You're such a super lady.
jokes.setup: Lou receives Tic Tacs from Elaine
jokes.punchline: Overly earnest gratitude with awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Four hours.
jokes.setup: Context of home movies of boyhood trip to Michigan
jokes.punchline: George's flat delivery of the excessive runtime
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with perfect economy

jokes.text: You know, I rip off the label... I can hardly tell the difference. We've officially bottomed out.
jokes.setup: Jerry talks about generic wax beans
jokes.punchline: Kramer acknowledges they've hit rock bottom for show content
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on show quality within the show

jokes.text: Your home, Mr. Costanza. Just make sure he gets his medicine six times a day... and keep his tail elevated.
jokes.setup: Vet discharging the squirrel
jokes.punchline: Treating wild animal like domestic pet with specific care instructions
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation of treating wild animal as pet

jokes.text: It's not a pet. It's a wild invalid. And it knows that I tried to kill it. As soon as it gets better, it's gonna gnaw my brain out in my sleep.
jokes.setup: Miranda suggests the squirrel might be fun as a pet
jokes.punchline: George's paranoid escalation about revenge-seeking squirrel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George paranoia with perfect escalation and great imagery

jokes.text: He's a human maraca.
jokes.setup: Context about Lou's Tic Tac rattling problem
jokes.punchline: Perfect metaphor for the constant rattling sound
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor with perfect economy - very quotable

jokes.text: You step on it and it fushes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of 30-year-old Easy-Bake Oven batter
jokes.punchline: Jerry's onomatopoeia for the sound of old batter
jokes.explanation: Good descriptive detail with invented sound effect

jokes.text: That reminds me of the Haitian voodoo rattle torture. You haven't gone over to their side, have you?
jokes.setup: Peterman hears Tic Tac rattling
jokes.punchline: Jumps to elaborate voodoo conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman paranoid worldliness with great character integration

jokes.text: Because if I hear one more rattle, just one, you're out on your can. And if you are undead... l'll find out about that too.
jokes.setup: Peterman's voodoo paranoia about Elaine
jokes.punchline: Threatens to investigate her undead status
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Peterman's paranoia with perfect character voice

jokes.text: The only gum I liked came with the Mickey Mouse gumball machine. They stopped making that 20 years ago. Well, stinky, this is your lucky day.
jokes.setup: Lou explains his gum preferences with specific childhood reference
jokes.punchline: Elaine's solution involves her having access to discontinued toy
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for toy connection but not particularly strong

jokes.text: It was great. I've done it a few more times since then.
jokes.setup: Kramer asks how Jerry feels about drugging woman for toys
jokes.punchline: Jerry doubles down, admitting to repeat offenses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Jerry even worse

jokes.text: What is this? What is she doing here? What? It's the new format. Scandals and animals.
jokes.setup: Celia appears on show to confront Jerry
jokes.punchline: Kramer explains his talk show format evolution
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about trashy talk show evolution

jokes.text: Well, what kind of woman drinks an entire box of wine?
jokes.setup: Jerry being confronted about drugging Celia
jokes.punchline: Jerry deflects by victim-blaming about wine consumption
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's terrible deflection strategy

jokes.text: No, you idiot. Hawks eat squirrels.
jokes.setup: Kramer wants to bring hawk and squirrel together for confrontation
jokes.punchline: Jerry points out the obvious predator-prey relationship
jokes.explanation: Sets up the disaster but fairly straightforward

jokes.text: Well, at least we know that prosthetic squirrel hips work.
jokes.setup: Aftermath of hawk attacking squirrel and destroying set
jokes.punchline: Jerry finds silver lining in successful prosthetics
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan callback that finds absurd positive in disaster

jokes.text: Oh, go ahead, pigeons. Laugh it up. I'm getting in my car now, and the last I heard, we have no deal.
jokes.setup: George addresses pigeons directly
jokes.punchline: Threatens to break the deal while acknowledging their mockery
jokes.explanation: Perfect George escalation that completes the pigeon arc

jokes.text: Oh, well, hey, I dated Jerry too. I know what a monster he can be.
jokes.setup: Celia complaining about Jerry
jokes.punchline: Elaine bonds over shared Jerry experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing female solidarity against Jerry

tmdb_overview: Kramer turns his apartment into a talk-show set; Jerry's girlfriend won't let him play with her toys; George's clash with pigeons leads to problems with a squirrel; a new employee's silent movement rattles Elaine.
